subtasks.description: Query TPC-H warehouse to identify top 10 customers in BUILDING market segment ranked by lifetime value (total revenue from all orders), returning customer ID, name, and lifetime value metrics.

Query TPC-H warehouse to retrieve order history patterns for the identified top 10 customers, including order frequency, average order value, product categories purchased, temporal distribution, and repeat purchase metrics.

Using the top 10 customer lifetime values and their order patterns, generate personalized retention narratives for each customer that reference their specific order behavior, purchasing trends, and engagement patterns to support retention strategy.

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks required: (1) Initial SQL to identify top 10 customers in BUILDING segment by lifetime value—mid tier for multi-table join with aggregation across orders. (2) Follow-on SQL for order pattern details (frequency, product mix, temporal trends)—mid tier for aggregation and windowing across order history. (3) Narrative generation—frontier tier because it requires synthesizing customer value data with behavioral pattern insights to produce personalized, contextual retention narratives that reference specific order behaviors. The frontier tier's reasoning capability is necessary to extract meaningful insights from order patterns and craft compelling narrative that connects customer value to retention motivation. Single-source (TPC-H SQL) so no cross_recon for reconciliation, but cross_recon specialist selected for the narrative synthesis task as it involves multi-signal reasoning and output generation beyond simple extraction.

output.sql: SELECT c.c_custkey, c.c_name, SUM(o.o_totalprice) AS lifetime_value, COUNT(o.o_orderkey) AS total_orders, AVG(o.o_totalprice) AS avg_order_value FROM customer c INNER JOIN orders o ON c.c_custkey = o.o_custkey WHERE c.c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING' GROUP BY c.c_custkey, c.c_name ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC LIMIT 10

WITH top_customers AS (SELECT c.c_custkey, c.c_name, SUM(o.o_totalprice) AS lifetime_value, COUNT(o.o_orderkey) AS total_orders, AVG(o.o_totalprice) AS avg_order_value FROM customer c INNER JOIN orders o ON c.c_custkey = o.o_custkey WHERE c.c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING' GROUP BY c.c_custkey, c.c_name ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC LIMIT 10) SELECT tc.c_custkey, tc.c_name, tc.lifetime_value, tc.total_orders, tc.avg_order_value, COUNT(DISTINCT o.o_orderkey) AS order_frequency, AVG(o.o_totalprice) AS avg_order_val, MIN(o.o_orderdate) AS first_order_date, MAX(o.o_orderdate) AS last_order_date, EXTRACT(DAY FROM MAX(o.o_orderdate) - MIN(o.o_orderdate)) AS customer_tenure_days, COUNT(DISTINCT DATE_TRUNC('month', o.o_orderdate)) AS months_active, ROUND(COUNT(DISTINCT o.o_orderkey)::NUMERIC / NULLIF(EXTRACT(DAY FROM MAX(o.o_orderdate) - MIN(o.o_orderdate))::NUMERIC / 30, 0), 2) AS orders_per_month, COUNT(CASE WHEN o.o_orderstatus = 'F' THEN 1 END) AS completed_orders, COUNT(CASE WHEN o.o_orderstatus = 'P' THEN 1 END) AS pending_orders, ROUND(AVG(l.l_quantity), 2) AS avg_line_quantity, COUNT(DISTINCT l.l_linenumber) AS total_line_items FROM top_customers tc INNER JOIN orders o ON tc.c_custkey = o.o_custkey LEFT JOIN lineitem l ON o.o_orderkey = l.l_orderkey GROUP BY tc.c_custkey, tc.c_name, tc.lifetime_value, tc.total_orders, tc.avg_order_value ORDER BY tc.lifetime_value DESC
